sn35.108:1.1: "Bhikkhus, when what exists, by clinging to what, by adhering to what, does the thought occur: ‘I am superior’ or ‘I am equal’ or ‘I am inferior’?"
sn35.108:14.1: "But without clinging to what is impermanent, suffering, and subject to change, could the thought occur: ‘I am superior’ or ‘I am equal’ or ‘I am inferior’?"
sn35.108:16.1: "Seeing thus … He understands: ‘ … there is no more for this state of being.’"sn.iv.89
sn35.108:2.1: "Venerable sir, our teachings are rooted in the Blessed One … ."
